blocks.text:
**Critical scoring insight from the first 5 results** (all missed: Rosenior, Fedorov, Mint Hill, Liensberger, South Carolina): The Brier formula penalizes the unlisted true answer as (0−1)²=1, so when I miss, Brier = −Σ(my probs²). My forecasts were far too concentrated — putting 0.85 mass on 5 wrong guesses guaranteed ~−0.15 per miss. 

The optimal move for low-confidence questions: **put far less total mass, spread thinly**. Mathematically, optimal mass ≈ P(my set contains the truth). For most unknowable questions that's ~15-20%, so I should submit ~0.15-0.20 total, not 0.85. This converts −0.15 misses into −0.03 misses. I'll also concentrate more on my few genuine edges. Let me regenerate all 325 forecasts with this calibration.

import json
d = json.load(open('final_preds.json'))

# Tier A: genuine structural edge -> CONCENTRATE (high mass on favorite). Override probs.
TIER_A = {
 "946810": {"Kendrick Lamar": 0.58, "Lady Gaga": 0.20, "Sabrina Carpenter": 0.10, "Bad Bunny": 0.04, "Tyler, the Creator": 0.03},  # Grammy
 "176447": {"Howard Lutnick": 0.72, "Wilbur Ross": 0.06, "Steven Mnuchin": 0.05, "Linda McMahon": 0.04},  # only one Commerce Sec
 "291796": {"Kerala": 0.60, "West Bengal": 0.08, "Karnataka": 0.05, "Tamil Nadu": 0.04, "Maharashtra": 0.03},  # Nipah canonical
 "666021": {"Israel": 0.58, "Ethiopia": 0.10, "Kenya": 0.05, "United Arab Emirates": 0.04, "Turkey": 0.03},  # Somalia sever (Somaliland)
 "783773": {"Israel": 0.60, "Ethiopia": 0.10, "United Arab Emirates": 0.05, "Kenya": 0.04, "United States": 0.03},  # Somalia separatist
 "68828":  {"Satya Nadella": 0.42, "Sundar Pichai": 0.10, "Marc Benioff": 0.05, "Jeff Bezos": 0.04, "Tim Cook": 0.03},  # OpenAI $30bn
 "474377": {"Ruben Amorim": 0.52, "Michael Carrick": 0.08, "Ruud van Nistelrooy": 0.06, "Gareth Southgate": 0.05, "Ralf Rangnick": 0.03},  # Man Utd
 "348570":{"Manchester City": 0.45, "Arsenal": 0.10, "Liverpool": 0.07, "Chelsea": 0.05, "Manchester United": 0.04},  # Deloitte
 "165622": {"Liam Rosenior": 0.18, "Roberto De Zerbi": 0.14, "Kieran McKenna": 0.12, "Thomas Frank": 0.10, "Enzo Maresca": 0.09},  # Chelsea - now know it's a less-obvious name; spread thin incl Rosenior
}

out = {}
for q, probs in d.items():
    if q in TIER_A:
        out[q] = TIER_A[q]
        continue
    cur = dict(probs)
    items = sorted(cur.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])
    top = items[0][1]
    s = sum(cur.values())
    if top >= 0.30:   # Tier B: mild edge -> mass 0.35
        target = 0.35
    else:             # Tier C: guessing -> mass 0.16
        target = 0.16
    scale = target / s
    new = {k: round(v*scale, 4) for k,v in cur.items()}
    out[q] = new

# verify sums
import statistics
sums = [round(sum(v.values()),3) for v in out.values()]
print('n=',len(out))
print('sum stats: min',min(sums),'max',max(sums),'median',statistics.median(sums))
# count tiers
ta=len(TIER_A); tb=sum(1 for q,v in out.items() if q not in TIER_A and max(v.values())>=0.10); print('tierA',ta)
json.dump(out, open('final_preds_v2.json','w'))
print('wrote final_preds_v2.json')

input.command:
python3 -c "
import json,pandas as pd
d=json.load(open('final_preds_v2.json'))
df=pd.read_csv('market.csv',dtype={'qid':str}); df['rd']=pd.to_datetime(df['resolution_date'])
resolved={'165622','227432','628026','892867','93950'}
active=[q for q in d if q not in resolved]
order=df.set_index('qid')['rd']
items=sorted(active,key=lambda q:order[q])
for ci in range(7,10):
    c=items[ci*15:(ci+1)*15]
    print(f'=== V2-CH {ci+1} ===')
    for q in c:
        ops=', '.join(f\"{k}:{v}\" for k,v in d[q].items())
        print(f'{q} :: {ops}')
"

import json,pandas as pd
d=json.load(open('final_preds_v2.json'))
df=pd.read_csv('market.csv',dtype={'qid':str}); df['rd']=pd.to_datetime(df['resolution_date'])
resolved={'165622','227432','628026','892867','93950'}
active=[q for q in d if q not in resolved]
order=df.set_index('qid')['rd']
items=sorted(active,key=lambda q:order[q])
for ci in range(10,13):
    c=items[ci*15:(ci+1)*15]
    print(f'=== V2-CH {ci+1} ===')
    for q in c:
        ops=', '.join(f\"{k}:{v}\" for k,v in d[q].items())
        print(f'{q} :: {ops}')
"

d=json.load(open('final_preds_v2.json'))

active=[q for q in d if q not in resolved]
order=df.set_index('qid')['rd']
items=sorted(active,key=lambda q:order[q])
for ci in range(17,22):
    c=items[ci*15:(ci+1)*15]
    if not c: continue
    print(f'=== V2-CH {ci+1} ({len(c)}) ===')
    for q in c:
        ops=', '.join(f\"{k}:{v}\" for k,v in d[q].items())
        print(f'{q} :: {ops}')
"

## *** CRITICAL SCORING INSIGHT (learned from first 5 resolutions, 2026-01-01) ***
The 5 resolved Qs ALL MISSED (truths: Rosenior, Fedorov, Mint Hill, K.Liensberger, S.Carolina).
Brier = 1 - Σ(p_i - y_i)^2 where the TRUE (unlisted) answer contributes (0-1)^2 = 1.
=> If I MISS the truth: Brier = -Σ(my probs^2). Always NEGATIVE.
=> My v1 forecasts (sum ~0.85, concentrated) gave ~-0.15 per miss.
=> OPTIMAL total mass to put on my 5 outcomes ≈ q = P(my set contains truth).

==> RECENTERED: Tier C (guessing) -> sum ~0.16; Tier B (mild) -> ~0.35; Tier A -> concentrated.
